pi_items.source: GitHub release page https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2 and install script verification of download URL
OpenBCI GUI documentation at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/ confirms Expert Mode enables keyboard shortcuts including screenshot functionality
OpenBCI GUI documentation states: 'Take a screenshot of the GUI using lowercase `m`!' - https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/

OpenBCI GUI documentation specifies screenshots are saved to '/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/'. Setup script (setup_task.sh line 29-30) confirms: '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory is used
Task analysis: requires only clicking GUI and pressing one key; task description is clear and straightforward

privileged_info_summary: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 is a real, publicly available application that includes Expert Mode functionality enabling keyboard shortcuts for screenshots. The 'm' key saves screenshots to the user's OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots directory. The output location is more specifically ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/ rather than the broader ~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/ directory stated in metadata. The task uses synthetic EEG data (no real subject data) and is straightforward in complexity.
